name: Tallinn
summary: Tallinn is the capital of Estonia, a small country on the chilly Baltic Sea. It is famous for its walled medieval Old Town with red-roofed towers, cobbled lanes and a tall pink castle on a hill.
funFacts: Tallinn's Old Town is more than 800 years old and still has thick stone walls and around 26 round towers, like a real-life fairy tale castle town.
On Town Hall Square stands Raeapteek, the oldest pharmacy in Europe still working today — it has been selling medicines since the year 1422.
Skype, the app that lets people video-call each other for free, was invented by programmers right here in Tallinn back in 2003.
Estonian forests are full of brown bears, wolves and shy lynx — there are more bears in tiny Estonia than people in some big cities.
Estonia is so digital that grown-ups can vote in elections using a computer, and almost every kid learns about coding at school.
thingsToDo: Walk around the medieval Old Town walls and count the chunky red-roofed towers.
Climb Toompea Hill to see the pink castle and the tall Pikk Hermann tower flying Estonia's flag.
Step into the cobbled Town Hall Square and visit Raeapteek, Europe's oldest pharmacy.
Peek inside the huge Alexander Nevsky Cathedral with its black onion-shaped domes.
Climb the spire of St Olaf's Church for a sky-high view over the red rooftops.
Wander the flower gardens of pink Kadriorg Palace built by a Russian tsar long ago.

wildlife.name: Brown Bear
wildlife.description: Big shaggy forest bear that loves berries, fish and a long winter sleep.

wildlife.name: Eurasian Lynx
wildlife.description: Spotted wild cat with tufted ears that quietly hunts in Estonia's snowy woods.

wildlife.name: White Stork
wildlife.description: Tall white bird with long red legs that builds huge nests on rooftops.

wildlife.name: Grey Seal
wildlife.description: Whiskery sea mammal that flops on Baltic rocks and dives for fish.

localWord.meaning: Hello, in Estonian — say it like TEH-reh.

images.alt: A sweeping view of Tallinn's Old Town with red-tiled rooftops, church spires and stone walls.
The cobbled Town Hall Square in Tallinn with colourful old houses and the tall Gothic town hall tower.
The tall Pikk Hermann tower of Toompea Castle flying the blue, black and white Estonian flag.
The grand Alexander Nevsky Cathedral in Tallinn with its black onion domes topped by golden crosses.
The chunky round medieval tower called Fat Margaret guarding Tallinn's stone city gate.
The pink Kadriorg Palace in Tallinn with bright flower gardens and a fountain in front.
A shaggy brown bear running across grass.
A Eurasian lynx with spotted fur and tufted ears watching from the forest.
A white stork with long red legs standing in a meadow.
A whiskery grey seal resting on a rock in the Baltic Sea near Estonia.
